points.text: Metagenomi Therapeutics announced a publication in Nature Structural & Molecular Biology detailing a proprietary compact CRISPR nuclease with enhanced genome editing efficiency.
Metagenomi Therapeutics completed a pre-IND meeting for MGX-001, a CRISPR-based hemophilia A therapy, demonstrating curative FVIII activity in non-human primates.
Metagenomi Therapeutics rebranded to reflect its strategic evolution as an in vivo genome editing company utilizing proprietary CRISPR technologies.
Metagenomi Therapeutics reported $160.8 million in cash and equivalents as of December 31, 2025, providing operational runway through Q4 2027.
Metagenomi Therapeutics demonstrated in vivo proof-of-concept for Antithrombin (AT-III) Deficiency using its MGX-001 site-specific genome integration system in non-human primates.
Metagenomi Therapeutics presented preclinical data for APOC3 as a new collaboration target with Ionis Pharmaceuticals under a Wave 1 agreement covering four targets in cardiometabolic indications.
CRISPR-driven gene editing innovations are identified as a primary growth driver for the global cell therapy technology market forecast 2025-2030.
NIH-funded research achieved a breakthrough in shrinking CRISPR components to enable precision delivery within the human body.
Rockefeller University researchers used CRISPR gene editing to reprogram hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells (HSPCs) to produce broadly neutralizing antibodies (bNAbs) against HIV in mice.
Metagenomi Therapeutics — MGX-001 — Hemophilia A — Pre-IND meeting completed; curative FVIII activity demonstrated in NHPs; IND submission planned Q4 2026; clinical trials targeted for 2027.
Metagenomi Therapeutics — Proprietary Compact CRISPR Nuclease — Genome Editing — Publication in Nature Structural & Molecular Biology highlights enhanced editing efficiency and compact size.

Metagenomi Therapeutics — MGX-001 Platform — Antithrombin Deficiency — In vivo proof-of-concept achieved in NHPs, validating expansion of site-specific genome integration to secreted protein disorders.
Cell Therapy Market — CRISPR gene editing — Market Growth — CRISPR-driven innovations cited as key factor propelling cell therapy market expansion through 2030.
NIH — CRISPR gene editing — In vivo delivery — Developed method to reduce CRISPR size for improved systemic delivery; specific mechanism and trial status not disclosed.
Rockefeller University — CRISPR-edited HSPCs — HIV prevention — Engineered stem cells produced enduring bNAbs that neutralized HIV ex vivo in mouse models.
NIH-funded team — Miniaturized CRISPR — In vivo delivery — Developed reduced-size CRISPR systems to facilitate precision delivery throughout the body.

Rockefeller University — CRISPR-mediated stem cell reprogramming — HIV broadly neutralizing antibodies — Engineered immunoglobulin loci in mouse HSPCs to drive sustained bNAb production; strategy published in Science.

Metagenomi Therapeutics — Strategic evolution to precision genetic medicines — MGX-001 (Hemophilia A) + AT-III + APOC3 pipeline — $160.8M cash runway through Q4 2027; corporate name change effective April 2026.

Ionis Pharmaceuticals — Cardiometabolic collaboration — APOC3 target (Wave 1 of 4) — Metagenomi presented preclinical data at Nature Conference 'Cracking the Code: Nucleic Acid Medicines Coming of Age'.

MGX-001 — Mechanism: Site-specific genome integration via proprietary compact CRISPR nuclease — Phase: Pre-IND — Population: Hemophilia A (FVIII deficiency) — Results: Curative FVIII activity in non-human primates.

MGX-001 Platform — Mechanism: CRISPR-mediated site-specific integration — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Antithrombin (AT-III) Deficiency — Results: In vivo proof-of-concept demonstrated in non-human primates.

Proprietary Compact CRISPR Nuclease — Mechanism: Enhanced genome editing efficiency with reduced size — Phase: Preclinical — Population: N/A — Results: Publication in Nature Structural & Molecular Biology confirms technical advancement.

CRISPR — Mechanism: Gene editing via reduced-size components — Phase: Preclinical (implied by delivery breakthrough) — Population: Systemic in vivo application — Endpoints: Successful size reduction for precision delivery; specific efficacy data not reported.

CRISPR-edited HSPCs — Mechanism: Integration of anti-HIV bNAb instructions into immunoglobulin loci of stem cells — Phase: Preclinical (mouse model) — Population: Mice transplanted with edited HSPCs — Endpoints/Results: Production of large numbers of bNAbs; ex vivo HIV neutralization observed.

Miniaturized CRISPR — Mechanism: Size reduction of CRISPR components for enhanced delivery — Phase: Preclinical research — Population: In vivo delivery targets — Endpoints/Results: Successful shrinkage of CRISPR system to enable precision delivery in the body.

CRISPR-edited HSPCs — Mechanism: Immunoglobulin locus integration for bNAb expression — Phase: Preclinical (murine model) — Population: Mice transplanted with edited stem cells — Comparator: None stated — Results: Endogenous production of HIV-neutralizing antibodies; potential application for influenza, rare genetic disorders, and cancer.

Metagenomi Therapeutics — Strategic rebranding — Corporate name change to Metagenomi Therapeutics, Inc. — Reflects evolution from platform developer to in vivo genome editing company focused on curative genetic medicines.
Metagenomi Therapeutics — Financial position — Full Year 2025 — $160.8 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities as of December 31, 2025; runway extends to Q4 2027.
Metagenomi Therapeutics / Ionis Pharmaceuticals — Collaboration agreement — APOC3 (Cardiometabolic) — Wave 1 of 4 targets; preclinical data presented at Nature Conference.
